record.field: GLPI is a mature open-source IT Service Management and Asset Management platform that unifies your help desk, inventory and CMDB in a single self-hosted application.

GLPI is a mature open-source IT Service Management and Asset Management platform that unifies your help desk, inventory and CMDB in a single self-hosted application.

It combines ITIL-aligned incident, problem and change management with hardware and software inventory, license tracking, contracts and a configuration management database. Designed for IT departments and managed service providers, it scales from small teams to large multi-entity organizations and is extended through a rich plugin marketplace. Authentication integrates with LDAP, SSO and OAuth, with granular per-profile permissions.

Key features
  • ITIL service desk: ticketing, incidents, problems, changes and SLAs
  • IT asset management with dynamic native inventory and CMDB
  • Software, license and contract tracking for compliance and cost control
  • Data Center Infrastructure Management (DCIM) and rack visualization
  • Self-service portal, knowledge base and FAQ for end users
  • LDAP/SSO/OAuth authentication with granular role-based permissions
Best for

IT teams, internal help desks and managed service providers that need a unified, self-hosted solution for asset inventory, ITIL ticketing and configuration management.

services: glpi-mysql: image: mysql:9.1.0 restart: always volumes: - ./glpi_mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_glpi_mysql' networks: - internal glpi-web: image: elestio/glpi:10.0.16 restart: always volumes: - /etc/timezone:/etc/timezone:ro - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro - ./glpi_www_data:/var/www/html/glpi environment: - TIMEZONE=Europe/Brussels container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T glpi-mysql sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d glpi-mysql # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T glpi-mysql sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
